mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 20-day horizon. This mechanism assumes that consumer spending directly correlates with corporate profitability and investor sentiment.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.01%) when RSAFS shows consistent month-over-month growth for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, exceeds a critical threshold, it signals increased likelihood of aggressive monetary tightening by the Fed, which negatively impacts equity valuations by raising discount rates and increasing recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive on average when CPIAUCSL YoY change is above 5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and declining non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a severe deterioration in the labor market, signaling an impending recession that significantly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 returns negatively over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat (mean return < 0.01%) in more than 50% of instances where both UNRATE and PAYEMS triggers are met.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, as indicated by strong GDP growth (GDPC1), the market anticipates continued corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when growth is accelerating, reflecting strong underlying demand and business confidence.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average during periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a certain level, it signifies a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, which dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive on average when FEDFUNDS is above 1.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating strong underlying economic activity and corporate productivity, which generally supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, as investors anticipate higher future earnings.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.01%) when INDPRO shows consistent month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a historical average or natural rate signals an overheating labor market, potentially leading to wage inflation and prompting the Fed to tighten monetary policy, which can negatively impact S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive on average when UNRATE is below 3.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer and investment demand, suggesting a significant economic slowdown or recession, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat (mean return < 0.01%) in more than 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS triggers are met.
